commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4bf1467e6e44e63a45b25f03f6763cb882568522)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a `richMode` option to `markdownToTelegramHtml`, converts generated blockquote and heading tags to `` on the default path, and adds `
` to the legacy Telegram HTML allowlist. PR surface: Source +13. Total +13 across 1 file. Reproducibility: yes. from source and the linked user report: current non-rich Telegram reply rendering can produce `
` and sends it through `parse_mode: HTML`, while the linked issue includes real Telegram Web unsupported-message evidence. I did not run live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer code changes plus real Telegram proof before merge; the repair lane should not take over while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: [codex] docs(memory-wiki): clarify sources wrapper contract
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #69700.

Clarifies the Memory Wiki `sources/` contract so the docs match current lint,
ingest, compile, and search behavior:

- describe `sources/` as source evidence that can be either structured imported
  source-page wrappers or explicitly marked unmanaged raw Markdown
- document `<!-- openclaw:wiki:raw-source -->` for unmanaged raw Markdown kept
  directly under `sources/`
- preserve the newer Open Knowledge Format import docs from current `main`
- mirror the source-page and raw-marker wording in the `wiki ingest` CLI docs

## Why

The old plugin docs described `sources/` as "imported raw material" without
explaining how lint distinguishes structured generated imports from unmanaged
raw Markdown. Current Memory Wiki behavior supports both paths:

- managed imports keep identity, page type, freshness, and provenance in
  source-page frontmatter
- unmanaged raw Markdown is accepted as source evidence when it includes the
  raw-source marker near the top of the page
- unmarked bare Markdown remains visible to structure lint so accidental
  malformed source pages are not silently accepted

## Verification

- `node scripts/docs-list.js`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md`
- `node scripts/check-docs-i18n-glossary.mjs`
- `node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Memory Wiki documentation now describes both supported
`sources/` paths: structured imported source-page wrappers with frontmatter and
unmanaged raw Markdown marked with `<!-- openclaw:wiki:raw-source -->`.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch
`fix-memory-wiki-sources-docs` at head `226550b706`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/docs-list.js`; `node
scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`; `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs
README.md`; `node scripts/check-docs-i18n-glossary.mjs`; `node
scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout after
this patch:

```text
$ node scripts/docs-list.js
Listing all markdown files in docs folder:
...
cli/wiki.md - CLI reference for `openclaw wiki` (memory-wiki vault status, search, compile, lint, apply, bridge, and Obsidian helpers)
...
plugins/memory-wiki.md - memory-wiki: compiled knowledge vault with provenance, claims, dashboards, and bridge mode
...
Reminder: keep docs up to date as behavior changes. When your task matches any "Read when" hint above (React hooks, cache directives, database work, tests, etc.), read that doc before coding, and suggest new coverage when it is missing.

$ node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check
Docs formatting clean (662 files).

$ node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md
Docs MDX check passed (677 files, 3513ms).

$ node scripts/check-docs-i18n-glossary.mjs
# exited 0 with no output

$ node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
checked_internal_links=4458
broken_links=0

$ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
# exited 0 with no output
```

Observed result after fix: The Memory Wiki plugin docs now describe structured
managed source-page wrappers, the unmanaged raw-source marker path, and the
reason unmarked bare source Markdown remains lint-visible. The wiki CLI docs
now mirror that contract in the `wiki ingest` section. The docs tooling above
accepted the edited pages and the docs link audit reported `broken_links=0`.

What was not tested: No runtime Memory Wiki command was run because this patch
only updates public docs and the raw-source behavior is already covered in
current `main` by Memory Wiki lint tests.
target.author: spacegeologist
